articles.title: Weather Today [25 March, 2026]: Check IMD Forecast, Snow, Heat Across States & Rain Predictions for Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu & Ahmedabad - The Sunday Guardian
articles.full_text:
Weather Today (25 March 2026): Check IMD forecast for Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, J&K, Ahmedabad, Punjab, Hyderabad & UP. Get city-wise temperature, rain, snow predictions & sunrise-sunset timings.
Weather Today (25 March 2026): Weather conditions across India are shifting toward warmer days as summer gradually sets in. While northern plains are experiencing rising daytime temperatures, hill regions continue to witness chilly mornings. Several cities are already touching above 35°C, indicating an early heat build-up. Meanwhile, air quality concerns persist in major metros, adding another layer to daily weather concerns.
Hill regions, particularly Srinagar and nearby areas, continue to experience cold mornings with sub-zero temperatures.
Srinagar Weather Today: Minimum: -7°C | Maximum: 4°C
Light winds around 2.28 km/h are expected with cold conditions continuing. Snowfall chances remain low, but frost conditions are likely during early morning hours. Over the next seven days, temperatures may gradually rise to 10°C, suggesting slow warming but continued cold conditions.
Minimum: 21°C | Maximum: 34°C
Delhi is expected to witness warm daytime conditions with mild mornings. Wind speed around 5.57 km/h may offer slight relief. Temperatures could climb to 36°C in the coming days, indicating an early summer trend.
Minimum: 26°C | Maximum: 33°C
Chennai is expected to remain humid and warm with coastal winds. Humidity levels may increase discomfort during afternoon hours.
Minimum: 28°C | Maximum: 31°C
Mumbai continues to experience stable coastal weather. Moderate winds at 8.67 km/h may keep temperatures relatively stable.
Minimum: 25°C | Maximum: 36°C
Hyderabad is warming up steadily with dry conditions and temperatures may reach 39°C in the coming days.
Minimum: 19°C | Maximum: 32°C
Bengaluru and nearby areas are expected to remain pleasant and afternoon heat may increase but evenings remain comfortable.
Minimum: 21°C | Maximum: 35°C
Uttar Pradesh and surrounding areas are seeing rising temperatures may approach 40°C later this week.
Minimum: 22°C | Maximum: 33°C
Rajasthan is expected to witness warm and dry weather where winds may contribute to rising heat.
Minimum: -7°C | Maximum: 4°C
Cold conditions continue in the valley and clear skies with chilly winds expected.
Minimum: 17°C | Maximum: 30°C
Punjab weather remains pleasant but warming and temperatures may rise to 34°C soon.
Minimum: 25°C | Maximum: 37°C
Ahmedabad is already experiencing summer-like heat and dry winds expected during afternoon.
Rising temperatures could lead to increased power consumption, dehydration risks and worsening air quality where as outdoor workers and commuters may face discomfort during peak afternoon hours.
Delhi’s air quality remains Moderate to Poor with AQI levels fluctuating around 165-190 and sensitive groups may experience breathing discomfort.
AQI Scale Guide:
1. Are there any travel or safety warnings?
Travelers should avoid midday travel in hot regions and carry hydration essentials.
2. Will temperatures rise across the country?
Yes, most cities are expected to see rising temperatures over the next week.
3. Will there be any rain today?
Rain chances remain low across major cities.
4. Where will it snow today?
Light snow chances remain limited to higher altitudes of Kashmir.
5. Is heatwave expected soon?
Some western and northern regions may experience heatwave like conditions soon.
